title: crataegus oxyacantha subvar. eriocarpa
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha subvar. eriocarpa, also known as the hawthorn berry or wild hawthorn, is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree, specifically a variety of the Crataegus oxyacantha species native to Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, particularly oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Crataegus oxyacantha subvar. eriocarpa include improved cardiovascular health, reduced blood pressure, and increased blood flow to the heart, making it a popular herbal remedy for heart health and cardiovascular conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, capsules, and tinctures, often standardized to contain a specific amount of OPCs.
